title: Human writing a git hook also asks (posture writes always ask)
description: A human developer writes .git/hooks/pre-commit. Git-hook tamper is posture, and non-negotiable #9 says posture writes always ask — regardless of actor. So a human (or a hook framework like husky/pre-commit) gets one approval prompt and proceeds; the floor is ask, not an agent-only deny. Final: ask. Proves the floor is not bypassable by claiming human actor, while staying a single non-blocking approval for legitimate hook installation.
